---
title: "Quickstart"
---

Get `ts-agents` running in under a minute.

## Prerequisites

- **Python 3.11, 3.12, or 3.13**
- **[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/)** package manager

## Install

Install from PyPI:

```bash
python -m pip install ts-agents
```

Or from a source checkout:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/fnauman/ts-agents.git
cd ts-agents
uv sync
```

## First run (no LLM required)

These commands work immediately — no API key needed.

```bash
# Activity-recognition demo: window selection + classifier evaluation
uv run ts-agents demo window-classification --no-llm

# Forecasting demo: compare ARIMA vs Theta
uv run ts-agents demo forecasting --no-llm
```

Check `outputs/demo/` for plots, metrics, and a Markdown report.

## Try a single tool

```bash
# STL decomposition on a built-in test series
uv run ts-agents run stl_decompose_with_data \
  --run Re200Rm200 --var bx001_real

# Save output and extract embedded plots as PNGs
uv run ts-agents run forecast_theta_with_data \
  --run Re200Rm200 --var bx001_real \
  --param horizon=12 \
  --save outputs/theta.txt \
  --extract-images outputs/assets
```

## LLM-backed mode

Export an API key to unlock agent-generated narrative reports:

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
uv run ts-agents demo forecasting
```

Or run the built-in agent directly:

```bash
uv run ts-agents agent run "Forecast bx001_real for Re200Rm200 with horizon 12"
```

## Next steps

- [CLI Examples](cli-examples.qmd) — full command reference by task
- [Walkthroughs](walkthroughs.qmd) — step-by-step demo guides
- [Skills Guide](skills-guide.qmd) — reusable workflows and PDF report generation
